fields.title: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Black Series - 2023
fields.name: Mercedes-Benz AMG GT Black Series
fields.text: A Black Series. A tag only worn five times before, and even though the first time it appeared was on an SLK don’t let that detract from what it stands for. A Black Series Merc is a wild thing, lighter, faster and harder as the standard recipe goes, but somehow very much its own car – no-one else builds anything quite like a Black Series.

fields.title: Lamborghini Aventador SV - 2020
fields.name: Lamborghini Aventador SV
fields.text: The Aventador SuperVeloce is the sportiest Lamborghini ever, with a 750 CV V12 engine, carbon fiber body, and innovative suspension and steering systems. Learn more about its design, performance, and technical details on the official Lamborghini website.

fields.title: Lamborghini Reveulto - 2025
fields.name: Lamborghini Reveulto
fields.text: The Lamborghini Revuelto (Spanish pronunciation: [reˈβwelto]) is a mid-engine plug-in hybrid sports car produced by the Italian automobile manufacturer Lamborghini. It was officially unveiled on 29 March 2023 as a successor to the Aventador.[5] The Revuelto’s namesake is a Spanish fighting bull that fought in the arena of Barcelona in the 1880s.[6]

fields.title: Porche 911 GT3 RS -2016
fields.name: Porche 911 GT3 RS
fields.text: 911 GT3 RS highlights. The road-approved high-performance 911 GT3 RS sports car shows off its full potential on the race track. With a high-speed naturally aspirated engine, radical downforce and comprehensive lightweight construction. Porsche Active Aerodynamics (PAA) continuously adjusts the d…

fields.title: 2025 Porsche 911 Turbo S - 2025
fields.name: 2025 Porsche 911 Turbo S
fields.text: Porsche 911 Turbo's crazy-for-the-time speed, edgy, spinout-prone handling, and well-muscled, whale-tailed looks instantly earned it a spot on the list of exotic, car-enthusiast fantasy machines. The 2025 911 Turbo might seem less outrageous—the 911's shape is as familiar as a Wheaties box, after all—but its rocket-like performance and seductive driving character continue to deserve a place in the pantheon of go-faster automobiles. The current Turbo doesn't look much different from other 911s, but it is. Packed in its derrière is an ass-kicking twin-turbocharged 3.7-liter flat-six delivering 572 horsepower in the Turbo and a massive 640 ponies in Turbo S trim. That's enough for a 60 mph rip of 2.2 seconds and a quarter-mile run in 9.9 seconds at 138 mph, making the Turbo S one of the quickest vehicles we have ever tested.

fields.title: Lamborghini Aventador SVJ - 2024
fields.name: Lamborghini Aventador SVJ
fields.text: Lamborghini created the Aventador SVJ to embrace challenges head-on, combining cutting-edge technology with extraordinary design, while always refusing to compromise. In a future driven by technology, it’s easy to lose the genuine thrill of driving. But in the future shaped by Lamborghini, this won’t be left behind, because there will always be a driver behind the wheel.

fields.title: AMG GT 63 - 2023
fields.name: AMG GT 63
fields.text: The AMG GT 63 Coupe is a high-performance sports car with a handcrafted V8 biturbo engine, a 9-speed transmission and all-wheel drive. Explore its features, specifications, packages and pricing on the official Mercedes-Benz website.

fields.title: Ferrari SF90 - 2025
fields.name: Ferrari SF90
fields.text: The SF90 Stradale is the first Ferrari with plug-in hybrid electric vehicle architecture, combining a V8 turbo engine with three electric motors. It celebrates the 90th anniversary of Scuderia Ferrari and offers two versions: standard and sports.

fields.title: Koenigsegg Jesko - 2021
fields.name: Koenigsegg Jesko
fields.text: The Koenigsegg Jesko is a limited production mid-engine sports car produced by the Swedish automobile manufacturer Koenigsegg Automotive AB. The car was introduced at the 2021 Geneva Motor Show and was completely sold out before the show ended.[4][5][6] Succeeding the Agera, the Jesko is named as a tribute to the company founder's father, Jesko von Koenigsegg.[7] There are two variations of the car, the "Absolut" and the "Attack".[8]
